name: HbA1c — Prediabetes Range
message: HbA1c ≥ 5.7% indicates prediabetes range. Lifestyle intervention recommended.
actionRequired: Refer to diabetes prevention program. Retest in 6 months. Assess other cardiovascular risk factors.
sourceAuthority: American Diabetes Association — Standards of Diabetes Care 2024, Section 3

name: HbA1c — Diabetes Diagnostic Threshold
message: HbA1c ≥ 6.5% meets diagnostic criteria for diabetes mellitus.
actionRequired: Confirm with repeat test. Initiate diabetes management protocol. Refer to endocrinology if indicated.
sourceAuthority: American Diabetes Association — Standards of Diabetes Care 2024, Table 2.2

name: Serum Creatinine — Elevated
message: Serum creatinine > 1.2 mg/dL may indicate reduced kidney function.
actionRequired: Calculate eGFR. Order urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio. Review nephrotoxic medications.

name: Potassium — Hypokalemia
message: Potassium < 3.0 mEq/L — significant hypokalemia. Cardiac arrhythmia risk.
actionRequired: Oral or IV potassium replacement per severity protocol. ECG monitoring if symptomatic. Review diuretic therapy.
sourceAuthority: UpToDate Clinical Practice — Overview of the causes and treatment of hypokalemia, 2024

name: Potassium — Critical Hypokalemia
message: Potassium < 2.5 mEq/L — CRITICAL hypokalemia. Immediate intervention required.
actionRequired: URGENT: IV potassium replacement under continuous cardiac monitoring. ICU admission if unstable.
sourceAuthority: UpToDate Clinical Practice — Overview of the causes and treatment of hypokalemia, 2024

name: LDL Cholesterol — High
message: LDL cholesterol ≥ 160 mg/dL — elevated cardiovascular risk.
actionRequired: Assess 10-year ASCVD risk score. Consider statin therapy per ACC/AHA guidelines. Dietary counseling.
sourceAuthority: ACC/AHA 2019 Guideline on the Primary Prevention of Cardiovascular Disease

name: TSH — Hypothyroidism Threshold
message: TSH > 4.5 mIU/L — possible hypothyroidism. Confirm with free T4.
actionRequired: Order free T4 and TPO antibodies. Assess clinical symptoms. Consider levothyroxine if confirmed.
sourceAuthority: ATA 2014 Guidelines for the Treatment of Hypothyroidism
